title: Stage 1: Tactical Sharpness (1400→1650)
description: Stage 1 of the Tactical Progression Trainer. Learn to spot forks, pins, discovered attacks, and double checks that players at 1400-1650 consistently miss.

intro.text: Welcome to Stage 1 of the Chess99 Tactical Progression Trainer. At the 1400-1650 level, games are decided by one-move blunders and missed two-move tactics. In this session, we study the core patterns you must see instantly.

segments.title: The Fork — Win Two Pieces at Once
segments.text: A fork attacks two or more pieces simultaneously. The knight is the master forker — it attacks in a unique L-shape that pieces can't simply block.

segments.title: Example 1: Knight Fork
commentary: White has a crushing knight fork available. Can you spot it?
Ng5 threatens the queen and attacks f7 — a royal fork is coming.
After Nxf7, white forks the queen on d8 and the rook on h8. A winning material gain.

segments.title: The Pin — Immobilize the Defender
segments.text: A pin ties a piece to a more valuable piece behind it. An absolute pin means the pinned piece cannot legally move — exploit this mercilessly.

segments.title: Example 2: Absolute Pin Exploitation
commentary: White ignores the pin on the knight and strikes in the center.
After the exchanges, Bxf7+ forces the king off castling and Qh5+ delivers a devastating pin on the f7 pawn.
This shows how pins compound — one creates another.

segments.title: Discovered Attack — The Hidden Weapon
segments.text: Moving one piece uncovers an attack by the piece behind it. When the moving piece also gives check or attacks something valuable, it becomes a discovered attack that is almost impossible to defend.

segments.title: Example 3: Discovered Attack Wins Material
commentary: The knight on f3 can step away to discover the rook's attack on the c6 knight.
The opponent's best response still loses material — the discovered attack leaves no good defence.
Always look for pieces that, when moved, unleash a hidden weapon.

segments.text: These three patterns — fork, pin, and discovered attack — appear in almost every game at the 1400-1650 level. Train them until you see them instantly. Visit chess99.com to practice 500 real Lichess puzzles featuring exactly these motifs.
